title: 2.4 Queues
subtitle: FIFO operations, circular queues, and applications

content.introduction: Queues implement First-In-First-Out (FIFO) ordering and are central to scheduling, buffering, and breadth-first exploration.

sections.title: 2.4.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define queue ADT operations (enqueue, dequeue, front, isEmpty), FIFO semantics, and typical overflow/underflow conditions.
examples: Model a print queue with arrival order preserved
Explain customer-service ticket processing as FIFO
Differentiate queue from stack by service order

sections.title: 2.4.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Explain linear queue, circular queue, and linked queue representations. Show pointer/index movement and why circular queues avoid wasted front space.
examples: Show wrap-around in a circular queue of capacity 5
Use queue in BFS level-order traversal

sections.title: 2.4.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Queue enqueue/dequeue/front are O(1) in proper implementations. Compare array circular queues with linked queues for throughput, memory overhead, and resizing behavior.
examples: Compare queue implementations under bursty traffic
Estimate extra memory of node pointers in linked queues
Prefer circular array for bounded high-throughput buffers

keyTakeaways: Understand queue ADT and FIFO guarantees
Implement and reason about circular queue indices
Apply queues to BFS and scheduling pipelines
Choose representation based on capacity and workload
